column26: It was difficult to book any sort of event when on dark mode on the app, due to it not being properly implemented. The process for deleting accounts was also slightly more 'out-of-the-way' than it should have been, being grouped in under the privacy policy section despite the description of the tab having no indication that it would be in there.
column27: The calculator idea is innovative and worked well, I was pleasantly surprised by the presence of a unique and useful feature. The dark mode and high-contrast modes were unfortunately buggy, with dark mode presenting significant challenges for app usage. I was not expecting, but did appreciate, the splitting of articles into navigatable parts to make information more digestible.
column28: I think it is rather clever, providing a good cover-up if someone were to look at your home-screen. I wouldn't feel nervous if someone picked up my phone, unless they ended up seeing two different calculator apps (even in that case, they would likely never think anything of it). It is an inconspicuous method of disguise that allows the application to blend in well with the homescreen.
column29: I appreciate the splitting into different sections, since it organises information into specific sections that allowed me to choose particular parts without having to scroll and sift through the article in its entirety.
column30: Not really, It just served as an example to do things that I would likely do anyways if i was using the app as myself.
column31: Add a wider range of accessibility views (e.g. colour-blindness accomodation) as well as a centralised homepage showing only the most important information. I would also change the organisation of the different settings areas for ease of navigation. Another issue that i would change is to use different icons for the lock option due to it being easily confused with the parental controls icon. The lock icon is formatted exactly the same as the navigation buttons, meaning it is very easy for someone to accidentally click on it thinking that it's another navigation button to something like the privacy section. Positioning it somewhere else in the screeen and/or providing colour contrast between it and the other buttons would help alleviate this issue.
column32: A chatbot to help new users by answering basic queries but without the capabilities to do anything that could go wrong and negatively impact the user experience.
column33: the task descriptions could maybe have been more vague to better test how intuitive the UI was
